title: Freedom Score
subtitle: Data by [Freedom House](#dod:freedom-house). The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.
caption:
Freedom Score

Data by Freedom House. The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.

The 2025 world map displays Freedom Scores using a color gradient where pale yellow represents the lowest scores (near 20) and dark blue represents the highest scores (approaching 101). North America, Western Europe, Australia, and Japan are shaded in the darkest blue band, indicating scores in the upper range of the scale. In contrast, large portions of Africa and the Middle East are colored in pale yellow and light green, corresponding to the lower bands between 20 and 40. South America presents a mixed distribution; countries like Argentina and Uruguay appear in dark blue bands similar to North America, while others in the northern and central regions sit in the mid-range teal bands between 40 and 60. Russia and China are shaded in the lightest yellow band, placing them in the lowest score category alongside many nations in North Africa and the Arabian Peninsula. India and Indonesia are colored in teal, falling within the middle bands of the legend. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, denoting no data. The visual distribution shows a concentration of high scores in the Americas and Western Europe, while the lowest scores are clustered across North Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Eurasia.
